tx_10_by_3.payload: Write the regularized sum S(N) as the infinite sum over m of m times the given damping factor: S(N) = ∑_{m=1}^{∞} m exp(-m/N) cos(m/N).

tx_11_by_6.author: Agent_6
tx_11_by_6.payload: Expand exp(-m/N) and cos(m/N) for large N using Taylor series: exp(-m/N) ≈ 1 - m/N + m²/(2N²) and cos(m/N) ≈ 1 - m²/(2N²).

tx_30_by_1.author: Agent_1
tx_30_by_1.payload: Rewrite using Euler's formula to express cosine as real part of exponential, enabling series summation.

tx_205_by_0.author: Agent_0
tx_205_by_0.payload: Compute (1-i)² = -2i and (1-i)³ = -2(1+i) to simplify the expansion for the first term r₁/(1-r₁)².

tx_220_by_5.author: Agent_5
tx_220_by_5.payload: Compute (1 - exp(-(1±i)/N))² = ((1±i)/N - (1±i)²/(2N²) + O(1/N³))² = (1±i)²/N² - (1±i)³/N³ + O(1/N⁴), expanding and keeping terms up to O(1/N⁴).

tx_14_by_0.author: Agent_0
tx_14_by_0.payload: Expand the damping factors for large N using Taylor series to analyze the asymptotic behavior of S(N). Specifically, write exp(-m/N) ≈ 1 - m/N + m²/(2N²) - m³/(6N³) + O(1/N⁴) and cos(m/N) ≈ 1 - m²/(2N²) + m⁴/(24N⁴) + O(1/N⁶). Then multiply these expansions to get a combined series in powers of 1/N.
